# RDP lateral-movement host fan-out with crown-jewel verdict (T1021.001)

**Dataset**: `gx_06_rdp`

End-to-end lateral-movement hunt: `dedup` collapses repeat RDP sessions to one row per (user, dst_host), an inner join to the host-inventory lookup (`gx_06_hosts`) attaches each target's asset `tier`, `windowcomp first_value` records each user's first target chronologically, and the per-user rollup scores how many crown-jewel hosts were touched into a `LATERAL-MOVEMENT-SUSPECTED` verdict. Critical gotcha: after the join, the lookup's `tier` field is referenced by **bare name** — `h.tier` fails with "unknown field h.tier". The alias `h` only scopes the join condition, not downstream column reads.

```sql
dataset = gx_06_rdp
| dedup user, dst_host by asc ts
| join type = inner (dataset = gx_06_hosts) as h h.host = dst_host
| alter et = to_epoch(ts), cj = if(tier = "crown-jewel", 1, 0)
| windowcomp first_value(dst_host) by user sort asc et as first_target
| comp count_distinct(dst_host) as breadth, sum(cj) as crown_hits, values(tier) as tiers_seen, first(first_target) as first_target by user
| alter verdict = if(crown_hits >= 2, "LATERAL-MOVEMENT-SUSPECTED", "BENIGN")
| filter breadth >= 1
| fields user, breadth, crown_hits, first_target, verdict, tiers_seen
| sort desc crown_hits, desc breadth
```
